programme.title: Léon: The Professional
programme.desc: After her father, step-mother, step-sister and little brother are killed by her father's employers, the 12-year-old daughter of an abject drug dealer manages to take refuge in the apartment of a professional hitman who at her request teaches her the methods of his job so she can take her revenge on the corrupt DEA agent who ruined her life by killing her beloved brother.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: After being snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, while the tyrants Dementus and Immortan Joe fight for power and control, the young Furiosa must survive many trials as she puts together the means to find her way home.

programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, Dracula's tortured henchman, is forced to capture prey for his master and do his every bidding. But now, after centuries of servitude, Renfield is ready to see if there is a life outside his boss's shadow.

programme.title: Son of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Returning to the ancestral castle long after the death of the monster, the son of Dr. Frankenstein meets a mad shepherd who is hiding the comatose creature. To clear the family name, he revives the creature and tries to rehabilitate him.

programme.title: Soylent Green
programme.desc: A nightmarish futuristic fantasy about the controlling power of big corporations and an innocent cop who stumbles on the truth.

programme.title: Terror Train
programme.desc: A college fraternity prank goes wrong and a student ends up in the mental asylum. Three years later, it's graduation time, and the members of the fraternity decide to have a costume party aboard a train trip to celebrate their graduation. Unknowingly to them, a killer has slipped aboard, killing them off one by one, disguised in the costumes of the victims.

programme.title: The Dead Don't Die
programme.desc: The focus is on the sleepy small town of Centerville where the dead become undead.

programme.title: The Ghost of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Ygor resurrects Frankenstein's monster and brings him to the original doctor's son Ludwig for help. Ludwig, obsessed with the idea of restoring the monster to full power, is unaware that his various associates all have different ideas about whose brain should be transplanted into the monster's skull.

programme.title: The Invisible Man
programme.desc: A scientist finds a way of becoming invisible, but in doing so, he becomes murderously insane.

programme.title: The Invisible Man Returns
programme.desc: The owner of a coal mining operation, falsely imprisoned for fratricide, takes a drug to make him invisible, despite its side effect: gradual madness.

programme.title: The Invisible Man's Revenge
programme.desc: An eager scientist tests his new formula for invisibility on an escaped fugitive. When the formula works the criminal runs off to terrorize a family he believes cheated him out of a fortune years earlier.

programme.title: The Invisible Ray
programme.desc: Dr. Janos Rukh discovers a certain type of radium that has almost magical healing properties. But the element has a dangerous side, too, and it has already started affecting Rukh. Consumed by paranoia, he begins to suspect that his wife is having an affair. Wild for revenge, Rukh hatches a deadly plot...using his own poisoned body as a weapon to kill.

programme.title: The Invisible Woman
programme.desc: Eccentric Professor Gibbs, brilliant but impractical, invents an invisibility machine and advertises for a guinea pig. What he gets is Kitty Carroll, an attractive, adventurous model, who thinks being invisible would help her settle a few scores. Complications arise when three comic gangsters steal the machine to use on their boss. But they fail to reckon with the Revenge of the Invisible Woman!

programme.title: The Little Shop of Horrors
programme.desc: A clumsy young man working at an impoverished flower shop discovers that the strange plant he has been nurturing has an insatiable appetite for blood, forcing him to kill to feed it.

programme.title: The Monkey
programme.desc: When twin brothers Bill and Hal find their father's old monkey toy in the attic, a series of gruesome deaths start. The siblings decide to throw the toy away and move on with their lives, growing apart over the years.

programme.title: The Monolith Monsters
programme.desc: Rocks from a meteor which grow when in contact with water threaten a sleepy Southwestern desert community.

programme.title: The Mummy
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian priest named Imhotep is revived when a British archaeological expedition finds his mummy and one of the researchers accidentally reads an ancient life-giving spell. Imhotep escapes from the field site and searches for the reincarnation of the soul of his lover.

programme.title: The Mummy's Curse
programme.desc: An irrigation project in the rural bayous of Louisiana unearths living mummy Kharis, who was buried in quicksand twenty-five years earlier.

programme.title: The Mummy's Ghost
programme.desc: An Egyptian high priest travels to America to reclaim the bodies of ancient Egyptian princess Ananka and her living guardian mummy Kharis. Learning that Ananka's spirit has been reincarnated into another body, he kidnaps a young woman of Egyptian descent with a mysterious resemblance to the princess. However, the high priest's greedy desires cause him to lose control of the mummy.

programme.title: The Mummy's Hand
programme.desc: A couple of comical, out-of-work archaeologists (Dick Foran and Wallace Ford) in Egypt discover evidence of the burial place of the ancient Egyptian princess Ananka. After receiving funding from an eccentric magician (Cecil Kellaway) and his beautiful daughter (Peggy Moran), they set out into the desert only to be terrorized by a sinister high priest (George Zucco) and the living mummy Kharis (Tom Tyler) who are the guardians of Ananka^Òs tomb.

programme.title: The Mummy's Tomb
programme.desc: A high priest travels to America with a living mummy to kill those who had desecrated the tomb of an Egyptian princess thirty years earlier.

programme.title: Aerosmith Live Osaka, Japan 1999 Millennium Countdown 2000
Paul McCartney Back In The US
Lennon Legend - The Very Best Of John Lennon
Led Zeppelin Live Royal Albert Hall
The Who - Live At The Isle Of Wight Festival (1970)
ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: One Flew Out of the Cuckoo's Nest (1)
programme.desc: Blanche's uncle Lucas visits Miami and has dinner with Dorothy. After learning of the unflattering descriptions of her in Blanche's letters, they decided to get even by pretending to be in love.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.sub-title: Señor Stinky Learns Absolutely Nothing About Life
programme.desc: The Golden Palace is being sued by the owner of another hotel: he says part of the girls' parking lot is on his property.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred proposes marriage to Donna Harris, a widow whom Fred is in love with. Lamont can't understand why a practical nurse in her 40's would be interested in a 65-year old junk dealer, and does his best to oppose their impending nuptials.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: A plumber's assistant causes Archie agony when he finds out the worker is a convict on a work-release program from Sing Sing.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: The Bunkers bid fond farewell to the Jeffersons when their neighbors abandon Queens for the nouveau-rich life in a Manhattan high rise.[This episode was the pilot for ""The Jeffersons.""]

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike and Gloria are dumbfounded by Edith's continual submissiveness to Archie. Gloria finally decides it's time to give her mother a long-overdue lesson in how to become more assertive in her marriage.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Vicki & Terrie vs. Jason & Teresa vs. Cynthia & Gary

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Shawn & Regina vs. Constance & Julie vs. Jody & Jennifer

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are willed a lot of dough from a rich uncle, but the executor of the estate, Icabob Slipp, is a crook who absconds with the money. The stooges trail him to a a theater where they engage in a wild chase and ultimately recover their inheritance.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in Elizabethan times, the stooges help their friend Cedric the Blacksmith win the hand of the fair princess Elaine. The only problem is that Elaine is promised to the Black Prince who is plotting to take over the kingdom. The stooges manage to foil the plot and the grateful King allows Cedric to marry Elaine.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are potters in ancient Rome during the reign of Emperor Octopus Grabus. When the emperor orders all beautiful red-headed women to be brought before him so he can select a wife, Diana, a pretty red-head, seeks refuge with the stooges. Some soldiers find Diana's hiding place and they are all brought to the palace where the stooges escape and try to pass of Curly as Diana, having broken the emperor's glasses. Their ruse fails and they're caught by the palace guards as they try to escape.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges accidentally invent a powerful new scotch while helping a bootlegger.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges' friend Jerry convinces them to buy an insurance policy on Curly and then have him act insane to collect. Moe and Larry put Curly on a leash and take him to the insurance doctor and have him act like a dog. But the plan backfires when the doctor wants to perform a brain operation on Curly.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Moe, Larry and Curly are three out-of-work tramps who are hired to promote a university football team. They're soon mistaken for the school's famous star athletes, "The Three Horsemen." As the star athletes, the Stooges are hired by a gangster to secretly play on his professional team, but of course the boys know nothing about football and bring their own set of skills to the game.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Joe's accidental snapshot of a paper plate blown by a breeze, is mistaken for a picture of a UFO.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After Police Officer Jackie gets slightly injured, Gary says he's tired of always worrying about her and wants her to quit her job as a Police Woman. Meanwhile, Becky and Darlene find something disturbing under D.J.'s bed that makes them believe he's a complete psycho.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky's folks pressure her to stop dating a boy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne, mistaken for a guy in her lumberjack Halloween costume, plays along with some braggarts and learns about machismo.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's PMS day and Dan's birthday are on a collision course.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky is suspended for allegedly making an obscene gesture in her class photo which makes her suddenly popular at school, but Roseanne is the one called into the principal's office.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan ill-advisedly tells Roseanne he's having dreams of a mysterious, and oddly homely, other woman.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Do You Know Where Your Parents Are?
programme.desc: Since the girls continually break curfew, Dan and Roseanne celebrate the anniversary of their first lovemaking by revisiting the restaurant/hotel and staying out very, very late, hoping to teach the girls a lesson, with the help of Officer Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: An angry and deflated Roseanne discovered that her mother always thought sister Jackie had more potential.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr.Grace has informed the ladies and gentleman's department that he wishes for them to compete in the annual ballroom dancing competition. With the direction of Mr.Humphries the staff learn some of the basic moves on the dance floor. And then with their fancy top hats and tails, they have a final rehearsal in front of Mr.Grace while he decides whether or not to let them in the competition.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr. Rumbold is going away temporarily on a business conference, and out of respect he suggests the staff allow elderly Mr. Grainger to take over in his place - but Mr. Grainger quickly becomes callous and abusive to his friends.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill attempts to trick Toony into going to the veterinarian, but Toony gets wise to Bill's plan and installs a trick of his own.
Featured cartoons: The Duck Doctor (1952) Guided Muscle (1955) Fish Tales (1936) Ventriloquist Cat (1950) Dr. Devil and Mr. Hare (1964)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An art critic, Dale Kingston, kills his uncle for his valuable collection of paintings. All the clues lead to the dead man's first wife. Can Columbo correct the Lieutenant's error before it is too late?

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude has lunch with a handsome, newly divorced ""friend,"" much against Walter's wishes.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude & Walter are having a dinner party with some friends. Walter's friend Albie is a fun-loving prankster who irritates Maude but makes Walter laugh. When Albie dies during an operation, Walter no longer feels safe.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy, Rose, and Blanche are three women who share a house in Miami, Florida. Dorothy's elderly mother, Sophia, unexpectedly moves in after her retirement home burns down, and Blanche accepts a proposal of marriage.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: Guess Who's Coming to the Wedding?
programme.desc: Dorothy's daughter, Kate, comes to Miami with an announcement. She's engaged to a podiatrist named Dennis. Dorothy is thrilled but her happiness quickly turns to anger when she realizes that ex-husband, Stan, is going to be invited. After 38 years of marriage, Stan had left Dorothy for a woman half her age. Dorothy threatens to make a scene at the reception which places Rose and Blanche in the role of peacemaker.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Having not dated since her husband died, Rose reluctantly agrees to go on a blind date. This date turns into a serious relationship when she is invited on a cruise which would involve sharing a stateroom.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: With Prime Time Wrestling emanating once again from the movie set of an upcoming western, Gorilla Monsoon watches as Bobby "The Brain" Heenan's antics lead him to take over as the film's new director. Plus, King Haku challenges The Ultimate Warrior for the Intercontinental Championship, Mr. Perfect goes one-on-one with Rick Martel, and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob's hero, a washed-up baseball player named Torpedo Jones, starts pitching for the local minor league team. Torpedo befriends Bob, but the dark underbelly of minor league baseball threatens to corrupt the Belchers.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Ray asks Debra why she ever married him, they take a walk back in time to the very beginning of it all. Ray is very nervous, he's about to propose to his lovely girlfriend Debra, and nothing is going right. When he finally pops the big question, she accepts, the families celebrates, and all is well, for about five minutes.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: While the Barones prepare for a friend's wedding, Ray kiddingly asks Debra, "Why did you marry me?" When she hesitates, he flashes back to the not-so-magical night 10 years prior, when he popped the big question.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug calls Arthur a demented old circus monkey and Arthur overhears it. Later Carrie is doing an impression of him and he walks in on that. To make up for it Doug decides to bring Arthur to a Mets game he won tickets for after being named employee of the month. While trying to make Arthur happy both Carrie and Doug wind up in jail.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie, along with Deacon and Kelly, go on a weekend trip. While there, they find out that Deacon and Kelly have a very active sex life.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George buys a convertible he thinks was once owned by Jon Voight. Kramer tries to save a small shoe-repair business. Elaine answers a radio quiz, enabling Mr. Pitt to participate in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade. Meanwhile, Jerry tries to solve the mystery of whether or not he's invited to Tim Whatley's annual Pre-Thanksgiving party.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Clothing problems are a hang-up; George hires a secretary.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne worries that Darlene may be planning to run away from home; new neighbor George befriends D.J.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is suffering from empty-nest syndrome as Darlene heads for school. But after Darlene gets settled into her dorm, the two have a revealing mother-daughter chat, and Roseanne returns home with a plan.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael feels threatened when Ryan returns to the Scranton office to share his ideas about the future of Dunder Mifflin. Jim and Pam's relationship is shared with the entire office, while Angela and Dwight's relationship is on the rocks.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After accidentally killing a nesting bird, Bart looks after her eggs, but when they hatch the babies are not fledgelings but tree lizards, who deposit their own eggs in birds' nests. They are quite unattractive but Bart takes to them, giving them names. They do, however, come into their own when it comes to ridding Springfield of its pigeon problem.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In "Hell Toupée," Snake is sent to the electric chair, and his hair is transplanted onto Homer. In "The Terror of Tiny Toon," Bart and Lisa become cartoon characters in an episode of Itchy & Scratchy. In "Starship Poopers," a growth spurt casts doubt on Maggie's paternity, and the family goes on the Jerry Springer show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: When You Dish Upon a Star
programme.desc: After a chance encounter with Hollywood celebrities secretly living in Springfield, Homer becomes their friend and gofer... until he spills the beans to the gang at Moe's.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Needing to know his middle name for form-filling purposes, Homer visits Seth and Munchie,two ageing hippies who knew his mother,at their farm and decides he too wants to be a hippy, Unfortunately he succeeds in ruining their organic juice-making apparatus and,to atone,makes juice of his own,using the hippies' supply of personal vegetables. This leads to Homer hallucinating and a run-in with the police.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Stuck at home with a cold, Lisa gets addicted to a video game and neglects her homework. Her A+++ on a test wins the school a grant, but when she confesses that she cheated, Skinner covers it up. Meanwhile, Homer raises a lobster.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: A toothache forces Al to see Marcy's dentist, a divorced man who devotes as much of his time to his work as he does to his young female dental assistant. At the same time, Al pressures Peggy to cook him just one square meal.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: He Ain't Much, But He's Mine
programme.desc: Peggy talks to a woman at a beauty parlor, and becomes convinced that Al is cheating on her, with that woman.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The Bundys' latest money making scam is to host a foreign exchange student in their garage. But the French beauty (Milla Jovovich) proves to be so popular that she ends up stealing all of Kelly's boyfriends.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When a Playboy Playmate walks into Al's shoe store, he's eager to get home and look up her October, 1987 issue. But Al is a broken man when he finds out that Peggy has sold his entire Playboy collection to get money to buy a good luck charm to win the lottery. The ghost of Al's father later appears and convinces him to get some self-respect.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Steve risks his job when Al takes a $50,000 loan from Steve's bank to start a shoe hotline.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When the Dodge finally gives its death rattle, Al digs up his secret car-fund shoe box and trades the Dodge for a ride to the local dealership. When Bud opens the shoe box, he finds not $5,000, but a mere $800... and a red hair. Al spends the rest of the episode trying to buy something that goes "vroom!"

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Steve spends time at the zoo with the Bundys instead of looking for a job, which upsets Marcy.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: With Fred in St. Louis, Grady has been put in charge of taking care of his garden. Grady quickly notices a strange plant growing and assumes that it's wild parsley. However, Rollo is convinced it's marijuana and Lamont decides to turn it into the police. However, when Smitty and Hoppy arrive, the plants are gone but Grady has made dinner for everyone, a nice big salad for everyone.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: After Lamont's friend Sandra takes Fred and Lamont to an art museum, Fred finds out that he can make art out of the junk in his pile. Thus, Fred begins the creation that will convey his legacy for eternity. However, the final result is far from an artistic masterpiece and Lamont has to decide how to break the news to his proud father.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Grady is getting too many Social Security checks in the mail and he is contemplating on whether or not he should be honest about reporting them or not. He finally decides the latter and keeps them, gambling them away in Las Vegas. Aunt Esther hits the ceiling when she learns of the situation and just then a phone call from the Social Security office comes into the Sanford residence.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Julio has gotten tired of the junk business and that's when Lamont comes up with an idea, Sanford & Son Salvage should merge with Julio's business. Julio goes for the idea but Fred is against it, but they merge anyway to form Sanford & Fuentes Salvage. However, everyone soon realizes the consequences attached to such a decision.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: After destroying her kitchen, Chris becomes Sharon's slave to work off the $2000 in damage and she also breaks his will.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: During one day, Chris meets his soulmate, marries her and goes through an entire relationship with her.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Books of literature come to life singing contemporary popular songs.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: An old couple recall an event and recall the gay 1890s.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Fairy-tale satire to the original Cinderella fairy-tale.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky ends up in a hallucinated dream about the dangers of smoking.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky travels over to the darkest Africa (in Wackyland) in search for the last existing Dodo - but he encounters strange and surrealistic creatures upon his discovery.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky spends an afternoon at the beach with his menacing nephew.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A story of holiday magazine characters coming to life engaged in musical songs.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Daffy Duck has been fired as a doctor's assistant and is desperate to find a patient to cure - ill or healthy.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head visit a coffee house; Beavis downs gallons of cappuccino and takes the stage as Cornholio.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Working the late shift at Burger World, the boys make up some special ""curly fries,"" find new a use for the ceiling fan and have a visit from the health inspector.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis mistakes a stranger for Butt-Head, and then can't get rid of him.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis meets his greatest hero, Fire.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The guys decide to start a band, get a gig, pick a cool name, break up and reminisce about it...all in one day.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head go to the courthouse to settle aparking ticket and wind up getting married.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The school librarian helps Beavis & Butt-head do research for a report on the woman they admire most... they admire her butt.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Anderson leaves his yard sale to run the local bar for a friend, so Beavis and Butt-head take over and sell, sell, sell...everything.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head attend a Parent-Teacher Association meeting and make a few suggestions on how to improve their education.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Principal McVicker gives the boys a pass to keep them away from school during President Clinton's visit, but they end up meeting him and becoming Students of the Year.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys discover they have the right to refuse service to customers at Burger World.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Miss Yvonne pays a visit to the Playhouse, yet Pee-wee is nowhere to be found. Miss Yvonne finds, instead, the words "For Sale" in front of the Playhouse abode. Shocked and confused, Miss Yvonne and everyone else try to remember all the better times that the Playhouse has gone through. Watch for today's secret "word." However, when Pee-Wee arrived to the playhouse about the "For Sale". But Pee-Wee realized that he is NOT selling the playhouse and has another sign that reads "LEMONADE". So the sign would had said. "Lemonade For Sale".

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: In this very first Playhouse episode, Pee Wee's friends, including Cowboy Curtis and Miss Yvonne, are introduced when they come over to swim in the pool. Captain Carl would be there too... if he weren't lost! When he calls in a mayday, it takes Conky and Globey to locate him and guide him to the delicious Sandwich Islands. Speaking of delicious, Pee Wee makes Ice cream Soup for the gang when rain drives them back through the Playhouse ""door"".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And Martha Stewart Have a Ball (1)
programme.desc: Max and Caroline plan to attend a big fashion gala at the Metropolitan Museum of Art where Martha Stewart is scheduled to be a guest, in an attempt to have her taste their cupcakes and get her seal of approval.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host another exciting and action-packed edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring a look at the recent issues between Hulk Hogan and WWE Champion "Macho Man" Randy Savage. Plus, The Rockers are in action, Ted DiBiase looks for a championship belt worthy of "The Million Dollar Man," and more.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon visits his wife in the Vale. Viserys and Rhaenyra broker agreements with the Valeryons. Alicent seeks the truth about the princess.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Jon and Daenerys arrive in Winterfell and are met with skepticism. Sam learns about the fate of his family. Cersei gives Euron the reward he aims for. Theon follows his heart.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.desc: Jaime is faced by his past mistakes. Tyrion's decisions are seen with doubt. The Battle of Winterfell is discussed tactically. Sansa and Danaerys discuss the future. The remaining Night's Watch are reunited. Everyone prepares emotionally with thoughts of their fate.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Foreman continues to experience the same progression of symptoms as the police officer. When he realizes he may be facing imminent death, he calls his father who comes to his son's side. As it comes down to a race against time, House believes the solution to the illness is in the police officer's apartment.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A young mother has a seizure while bathing her newborn son and nearly drowns him. House and the team have two cases at one time as they try to save the infant and determine the cause of the mother's seizures.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When an on-air remark catapults Bart to instant stardom, he finds that fame isn't all it's cracked up to be.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer participates in a hidden camera investigation of unsanitary food preparation at the Kwik-E-Mart, which costs Apu his job. Apu then enlists Homer's help on a journey to India to get his job back, while James Woods becomes the new Kwik-E-Mart clerk, as part of his research for a film project.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa's outrage over the gender stereotypes reinforced by a new talking doll prompts her to confront the dollmaker.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The powers that be at NASA, distraught over low television ratings for their space launches, decide to improve them by putting a private citizen on their next space shuttle. After receiving an angry phone call from Homer, they decide he is their man.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Flanders' extra ticket to the big game makes him Homer's new best friend, a role that sorely tests Flanders' patience.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After winning a phone-in competition Bart has a choice of prize of either $10,000 or an elephant, he chooses the latter, naming him Stampy. The Simpsons are in chaos after Stampy wrecks the house and eats all the food. They have no alternative, Stampy must go to an animal refuge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is picked to be Burns' heir and moves into the Burns mansion, prompting Homer and Marge to hire a deprogrammer to get him back.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart takes his dog to school, which gets Skinner fired, which in turn leaves Bart missing his archnemesis.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Boy Who Knew Too Much
programme.desc: After playing hooky from school, Bart sneaks in to Mayor Quimby's nephew Freddy's birthday party and witnesses a confrontation between Freddy and a waiter, Freddy is accused of beating the waiter but Bart knows what really happened, should he keep quiet or tell the truth and risk getting Cort playing hooky?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge arranges for her mother Jacqueline Bouvier to start dating Grandpa Simpson and all is going well until Burns cuts in and takes her away from Grandpa Abe. When he hears that she is getting married to Burns he goes to the church and,in a nod to the climax of the film 'The Graduate',runs away with her and,after she has declared that she doesn't want to marry either of them,they get on a bus together.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: The Undertaker, Kane, and Mankind compete in a No. 1 Contender's Match for the WWE Championship. DX takes their problems with The Nation to another level when they impersonate their rivals. The 1998 King of the Ring winner Ken Shamrock receives a royal welcome. Plus, Sable issues a challenge to Jacqueline, the Brawl for All continues, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: The once opulently rich Roses, following news that their now absent business manager has left them financially ruined, are left by the government with only a modest income and one asset, Schitt's Creek.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny must get permission to sell the town from its Mayor, Roland Schitt. David and Alexis spend a night out with the locals.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Oleg and Sophie’s plan to have Max and Caroline as Barbara’s godparents is derailed when Oleg’s domineering mother, Olga, arrives from the Ukraine for the baby’s baptism and fires Max and Caroline from their godparent duties.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline and Max are invited to speak about their business at Caroline’s alma mater, the University of Pennsylvania, Max takes the opportunity to give Caroline the college partying experience she never had. Also, Oleg’s attempts to bond with baby Barbara are rebuffed by a possessive Sophie, and Han practices his magic tricks with a reluctant Earl as his assistant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Earl becomes concerned he may not be able to keep up when his long lost sweetheart, Pilar, visits from Cuba, and wants him to take her sightseeing around the country. Also, Max must decide whether it’s time to take her texting relationship with Randy to the next level.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the girls take a bartending class in order to add cocktails to their dessert bar menu, Caroline must think quickly when she has to take the class exam solo after Max gets chicken pox from Han. Also, Sophie creates a creepy lookalike video monitor doll to keep tabs on baby Barbara.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's dessert bar business booms when they hire Clint, a popular bartender, but the girls need to intervene when Han becomes addicted to the mixologist's potent cocoa powder in his signature cacao drink. Also, Han enters a duck stamp-drawing contest, and glamour eludes Sophie as she adjusts to being a mom.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Randy and Max get tired of Caroline being the third wheel on their FaceTime dates, Randy sets her up with one of his co-workers. Also, Oleg is distraught when Sophie tells him to trade in his beloved car for a minivan.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When a hurricane approaches the area, Caroline fears that a big divorce party she's planning for a wealthy couple will get broken up when the unhappy pair get stranded in close quarters at the dessert bar. Also, Max worries that Randy really did break up with her when she suspects he’s blocking her calls, and Oleg is jealous when Sophie is stuck at home during the storm with their handsome new "manny".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Planes, Fingers and Automobiles
programme.desc: When Max decides to head to Los Angeles to win back Randy, she and Caroline embark on a cross-country road trip with Han at the wheel. Also, while Han and the girls are away, Oleg takes charge of the diner, and Sophie fills in as a waitress.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Riverboat Runs Through It
programme.desc: As Max and Caroline's road trip misadventures continue, their lack of money finds them stowing away and waitressing on a riverboat to New Orleans as Max tries to reach Randy in Texas. Back at the diner, Oleg and Sophie rake in big bucks when they sell pony rides with Chestnut.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get their big break when they’re cast as zombie extras after they finally arrive at the movie set in Texas where Randy is working. Back at home, Han and Oleg try their hand at construction after they fire the contractor working on the dessert bar.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline get home from their road trip, Caroline discovers she likes the dessert bar renovations as much as she likes Bobby, the contractor on the job. Also, Max rushes to Randy’s side when she hears he’s in the hospital in New York City, and Sophie joins a mommy group that cares more about partying than baby talk.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline is excited to go out on her first date with Bobby, but she has nervous jitters when she realizes Max will be alone and in charge of the dessert bar for the first time. Also, Sophie and Oleg decide to mix things up and go out on a date instead of staying in.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Caroline prepares for her third date with Bobby, she decides to spice things up by taking a burlesque class accompanied by a reluctant Max, who decides to give up sex in the wake of her breakup with Randy. Also, Max decides to take up a new hobby and goes golfing with Han.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Bobby invites Caroline to a party to meet his family, she is eager to make a good impression but isn’t prepared for the torch that his mother and sisters still carry for Bobby’s ex-girlfriend. Also, Caroline’s nerves are even more frayed when the diner gang decides to tag along.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Han's new therapist suggests that Max needs to confront her "daddy issues" and fear of intimacy, she reluctantly heads to Rhode Island to track down her birth father with the diner gang in tow. Also, Oleg is up in arms when Sophie wants him to help out around the house.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Baby and Other Things
programme.desc: When Caroline encourages Bobby’s sister, Denise, to quit her job, the couple heads toward their first fight after Caroline realizes the company Denise works for is her mother’s. Also, after Max reads a text on Bobby’s phone suggesting he has a child with his ex, Jessica, she and Han go undercover as an engaged couple to Jessica’s wedding planning shop to find out the truth.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: AFC Richmond play their final game of the season.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: American football coach Ted Lasso is hired by a wealthy divorcée to coach the English soccer team AFC Richmond.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.sub-title: To Save Us All from Satan's Power
programme.desc: A somnolent visit with the ghosts of Christmas past lands Tony back on Melfi's couch. With the annual pork-store holiday party approaching, Bacala reluctantly agrees to don the Santa suit that Pussy used to wear. Tony and Furio have a holiday surprise in store for the Russian who roughed up Janice.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.sub-title: For All Debts Public and Private
programme.desc: Tony invests in a land deal despite Carmela's concerns about money. Junior faces his own financial woes as his trial approaches. Adriana brings her new friend to the Soprano Sunday dinner, but Adriana has no idea who Danielle is working for. Tony bonds with Christopher by giving him the opportunity to avenge the murder of his father.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor puts Janet in her crosshairs to save Michael from eternal suffering.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael has a private meeting with Eleanor, who is put to the test. Also, Chidi harbors a secret and mulls over what to do.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael's skills are put to the test when he attempts to sort out an unprecedented situation. Meanwhile, Chidi makes a connection elsewhere, much to the dismay of Eleanor, while Janet shows a different side of herself.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Ten years later. Rhaenyra navigates Alicent's continued speculation about her children, while Daemon and Laena weigh an offer in Pentos.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin find out that Dad has been dressing as a woman to make friends.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin ends up in detention with Julius, who decides to make Darwin his new target. However, it backfires when Darwin ruins his life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin want to veg out all day, but their plans go awry and they have to save the rest of the family.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Tobias tries to be Gumball’s best friend.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The kids try to help Richard bond with Frankie.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Hot Dog Guy discover why they're so awkward with each other.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E01 - The Cage
programme.desc: The kids convince Mr. Corneille to become a cage fighter and set up a fight against a fearsome Russian fighter.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Alan has lost faith in humanity and turns the world black and white. Gumball must get over his hatred of Alan to bring the color back by singing.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: A group of kids get locked in the school after their parents have a charity gala. They appoint Gumball as leader to get them out but it leads to chaos.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E11 - The Anybody
programme.desc: When Clayton shapeshifts into Gumball to take his detention, he starts framing others for misdemeanors so Gumball and Darwin must track Clayton down to stop him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Can the Titans master the four New Year's traditions in order to break the cycle of bad years?

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin is determined to become a cat in order to get Starfire's attention.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After a humiliating defeat, Raven and the Titans work on building their lower body strength, becoming the "League of Legs."

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After Beast Boy gets money from the Tooth Fairy, the other Titans try to get in on the action by knocking out their own teeth.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After learning looks don't matter, Beast Boy undergoes a metamorphosis through a cocoon, becoming an ugly, gross creature.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Having grown sick of Robin's bland cooking, the other Titans make attempts to spice things up.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Stuck inside on a rainy day, the Titans do rainy-day activities to keep from boredom.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Hey You, Don't Forget About Me In Your Memory
programme.desc: When Robin decides to become an all-American class president-type person, he convinces the Titans to return to school with him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Accept The Next Proposition You Hear
programme.desc: Robin refuses to give the Titans any more orders so that they'll learn to think for themselves.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Control Freak informs the Titans that they are on a TV show he created and demands they earn him an award.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg's real strength comes not from his robot parts, but from his favorite song.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: When Ren falls ill, it's Nurse Stimpy to the rescue, swearing by the Sacred Bedpan to cure his friend. Will the constant care lead to a cured Chihuahua, or a total relapse?

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: During a 36-year mission to the Crab Nebula, Commander Höek succumbs to Space Madness and puts Cadet Stimpy in charge of the mysterious History Eraser Button!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy need new jobs, so they disguise themselves as Dalmatians, go to work for a psychotic fire chief, and battle a 40-story building fire, saving Mr. Horse and others!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this space adventure, Commander Höek and Cadet Stimpy land on an eerie planet with only their Space Cadet Handbook to guide them! Can they avoid certain doom? Nope.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Nature show host "Marlon" Höek and his sidekick Stimpy explore a strange Galapagos Island chain where all species look like Ren and Stimpy... but even less evolved!

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: McVicker sends Beavis and Butt-head to a group therapy session where they terrorize fellow patients.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to a women's rights meeting and try to hit on some militant feminists.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Highland High evacuates after receiving a bomb threat. Beavis & Butt-head stay, try to find the bomb, and cause some destruction of their own.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head plan to fake getting injured so they can get insurance money; real injuries follow.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Two babes want the boys to change their flat tire, Butt-head learns what not to do with a tire-iron.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Prep Boys (Kevin & Rich) vs. Slimes (Nikki & Traci)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Commercial Messages will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In the sketches, the cast does many parodies of TV commercials. They also try to sell some of Ross' malfunctioning products as part of another get-rich-quick scheme.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Walton's Mountain Becomes an Open Pit Mine will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast gets back to nature and make up the set like a farm.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Lassie Flunks Obedience School will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross decided to tutor the cast. He covers everything from reading to advanced chemistry.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
St. Elsewhere will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Everyone who has green slime dumped on them comes down with a mysterious flu.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Hulk Hogan vs. Punkey Brewster in Mismatch of the Century will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross gets the cast ready for the arrival of the cast of the Russian version of YCDTOTV.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
J.R. Ewing Saves His Money to Buy a Skateboard will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair and Adam compete over the new girl in the cast, while at the same time trying to save money for a car.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Miss Ugly Teen USA will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Matthew enters a contest at the local mall where he has to count jellybeans. Alasdair, not wanting to be outdone, enters a ""slime-in"" contest.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Censored Foul-Ups, Bleeps & Boners will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In an attempt to keep the show clean, the network censors have added bleeps and censor bars to the show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Lifestyles of the Poor and Unknown will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Naida's father hires the cast as servants after they all get fired.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
This episode features the exciting battle of the Airline Travel Industry against the Circus Workers. Vic discusses Kenny's options for joining the circus. Guy Le Douche's cousin Geek substitutes in for him so he can attend his annual parole hearing.
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Foul Balls; Little Man in the Boat; Corn Holders; Mudballs

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The fit and muscular physical fitness industry challenges the burned-out musical team in this MXC battle. Also, Kenny shows us some new game prototypes.
Games include: Turtle Gut Check; Mounting the Spike; Clear Sphere of Fear; Jerk and Release

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The fashion people take on the bible-thumpers on this edition of MXC. Also, Vic and Kenny continue their tribute to the new First Network for Men.
Games include: Yankers Away; The Poisonous Horny Toad; Shroom Zoom; Runaway Stump

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The adult entertainment industry challenges the home improvement industry in this edition. Captain Tenneal gets a special visit from porn star Ivana Swallow.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Dash to Death; Dope on a Rope; Elimination Idol

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Taking their chances in this episode, the gambling industry takes on the medical professionals. Also, we see how MXC is seen in the foreign nation of New Jersey.
Games include: Sack Lunch; Boulder Dash; Tight Enders; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
People with hobbies take on food service employees to see who's got what it takes to survive elimination. Kenny discusses his leisure activities, and the Captain shares his opinion on hobbies with the contestants.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Saddle Sores; Wallbangers; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Hi-Tech vs. Civil Service. Hi-Tech computer nerds take on the Government Civil Servants. It's a bombastic brawl pitting brilliance vs. bureaucracy, MXC style. Games: Dope on a Rope, Circle Jerkers, Swamp Gassers, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Cable TV Workers vs. White House Employees
programme.desc:
Finding out who's more ruthless: cable TV workers or White House employees. Meanwhile, Kenny remembers a White House tour.
Games include: Rotating Surfboards of Death; Door Jam; Mine Games; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Inspired by a lecture and the tribulations of Thomas Edison and the Wright Brothers, Mr. O'Neill encourages his class to pick something to fail at for personal growth. All his pupils get out of it instead are misery, grief, and despair, including Daria who may be forced to supervise Quinn at an upcoming mall fashion exhibit, and Jane, who's becoming so popular that she might replace Brittany as... a CHEERLEADER!!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While struggling to buy toilet paper for Jake, Daria gets stranded at the Lawndale High homecoming parade, where she meets Jane who plans to meet Tom so they can make fun of everybody. Instead, she runs into Tad Gupty who lost contact with his family(see "Pinch Sitter," Episode #1.8) and then Tom, who she finds is not such a bad guy after all. In fact, he may be more.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Helen has to cancel out of a business junket with Jake, guess who is coerced into taking her place?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Morgendorffers visit a "spa for the soul" to get their psyches massaged.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A labyrinthine discount-bulk-warehouse superstore serves as the destination for several characters. Jane goes there to buy Gummi Bears (for art supply purposes) after Tom ate hers. Daria accompanies her to buy new shoelaces, because Quinn stole the ones from her boot to repair a bag. Trent and Jesse go there to buy a headlamp and scented candles, respectively, and Tom accompanies them so he can find Jane and make up with her after their argument. (Walking with the two musicians, their half-brained purchase ideas drive him nearly up the wall.) The Fashion Club goes to the warehouse store to buy sunscreen and various other beauty needs, and, of course, their incredibly high standards prevent them from making any actual purchases. The free-sample cheese logs at the store become quite popular-an obsession, in fact, for hungry Mr. DeMartino, whose food budget is incredibly tight, and for Mrs. Johansen (a 300-pound hypoglycemic woman who is a recurring character). Kevin's dad, Doug, is holdi

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Is Lawndale a pretty little suburb, or an unholy nexus of evil haunted by the tortured denizens of the Beyond?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn discovers her spiritual side and we all have to live through it.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Jake suffers a slight kitchen mishap of the inferno variety, the Morgendorffers must move to a hotel.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Arch villain Silas Greenback has perfected his intergalactic traveling machine. Using Penfold as bait, the worlds most evil toad lures Dangermouse into his trap and boldly blasts him deep into time and space, towards a planet ruled by talking machines.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The rotation of the world is slowing down. So, DM immediately heads for the North Pole, where Baron Greenback is attempting to hold the world to ransom by stopping the world with a anti gravity rotation retarder of some sort.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Pyramids have been popping up all over London. It will take only twenty more to sink the whole country. So, Dangermouse and Penfold set forth for Africa, specifically the Saharah desert. After all, the sand the pyramids are made off must have come from somewhere.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse travels to outer space to find a custard mite and bring him to Earth after Greenback floods the world in instant custard.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Close Encounters of the Absurd Kind
programme.desc: Penfold is practicing Kung-Moggie when he and, more importantly, Danger Mouse are send to Eaves Drop Island (near the Bermuda Triangle) to safeguard the Big Ear tracking station from Baron Greenback. Once there, they are abducted by aliens and probed by Dr. Zock. However, DM refuses to believe Zock is nothing more than Greenback wearing a silly purple mask.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse is challenged to five tests of strengths of wit by Baron Greenback. If DM wins, Greenback promises to abandon his life of crime. If the Baron wins, the white wonder has to give up crime fighting.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: All over London, washing machines are breaking out of homes, laundrettes, warehouse and washing machine factories thanks to toad voice responder units build into them by the evil Baron Silas Greenback, who orders them to destroy Danger Mouse. DM counters by using a giant magnet against the machine, but does not count on the soap fuel's evolution into a hideous mutated sud monster.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Homemade Volcano, Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Walk Through Paper, Heat Spiral and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Homemade Volcano. Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Heat Spiral, Heat From your Body, Paper Fold, Computer Plane and Walk Through Paper.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Bird Flies, Bleach Art, Tricks with Gravity, Fire Extinguisher and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Fish Bombing Run, How a Bird Flies, Impossible Holes, Bleach Art, Woolly Aphids, Tricks with Gravity, Computer Explosions, and Home Made Fire Extinguishers.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Flour, Pendulum, Leaf Color, How a Siren Works, Blow out Candle, Guitar, Aurora, Crush can and more
programme.desc: n this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Flour Explosion, Pendulum, Hidden Leaf Color, How Sirens Work, Mapping a Guitar, Blown Out Candle, Card Illusion, Aurora Borealis, Come Back Can and Crush Gallon Can.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Speed of Sound, Refraction, Trick, Light Bulb Filament, Glass Duet, Spell Check, Stars and more
programme.desc: Speed of Sound, Selective Refraction Trick, How Many Stars, Glass Duet, Spelling Checker, After a Forest Fire, Window Oscillation, Asparagus, Finding a Tree's Height. Mr. Wizard measures the difference between hearing the sound of a starter pistol over the walkie-talkie and hearing it through the air.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman is asked about vaccinations, and calls upon Dr. Edward Jenner to explain how he came up with the idea. He later does a truck with water and a funnel and examines heat from friction.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains thermodynamics by showing how hot things cool and cold things warm up to room temperature, and how molecules move faster when they are hot than when they are cold. Afterward, Beakman explains why people get pimples.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman describes fossils and why they are important clues to learn what the world was like thousands of years ago. Dr. Louis Leakey explains that fossils are formed when plant or animal remains are quickly buried in sediment.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman demonstrates how people breath using a model of the diaphragm and lungs. Beakman also explains the sending of signals via telephones.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.sub-title: Tape Recordings, Beakmania & Force Vs. Pressure
programme.desc: Beakman demonstrates how cassette players convert sound into electromagnetic signals that get stored onto a tape and delves into the mystery of a bed of nails. Can you really lie on it without getting skewered?

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Actress Elaine Miles and Bill Nye explain why static electricity makes clothes stick together.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Feeling a little hungry? Then grab a snack and watch Bill Nye the Science Guy's episode on the Food Web. When it comes to eating, all living things depend on other living things. Take a chicken sandwich, for example. The bread came from plants. So did the lettuce and tomatoes. The cheese was made from milk, which came from a cow. To make milk, the cow had to stay alive by eating grass. The meat came from a chicken who once ate seed, and maybe the occasional bug. The animals that helped to make your sandwich depended on other living things to survive. The lettuce, grain (for the bread), and tomato got by fine on their own. Then some animal came along (you). Plants are the only big living things that don't need other living things to survive. All they need are sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water to make their own food. But it doesn't stop them from being eaten -- no way. In fact, plants are great things to eat. All animals need them in some way for food â€“ by the way,

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E12 - The Pact [SHDW, Mark SHaDoW]
programme.desc: Gumball and Principal Brown make a pact to tell each other what problems their girlfriends have.
